acceptance_targets: [CRAFT_PARALLEL_COLLISION_UNDERLIT] By the end of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:211, a cold reader can state 1) why Anya was at the compound, 2) what she has likely learned from Yor or the site, and 3) what new pressure or decision now exists between them. If those answers still require the next chapter, this finding fails.

[CRAFT_RECORDS_DISCOVERY_CONVENIENCE] In the revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:121 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:149, the reader can identify the obstacle Yor is racing, why it buys only a limited window, and what concrete risk or compromise she incurs by staying. If she still captures the entire packet under nothing more urgent than ambient radio noise, this finding fails.
[LW04-002] On a cold read, the first paragraph of Chapter 10 tells the reader where Yor's timeline sits relative to Damian and Anya's safe-house night before the compound action begins.
[LW05-001] By the time Anya appears outside Dominic's compound, the reader can say what happened after the safe house and why this new location follows from the prior chapter's promise.
[length_chapter_10_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:211, a cold reader can state 1) why Anya was at the compound, 2) what she has likely learned from Yor or the site, and 3) what new pressure or decision now exists between them. If those answers still require the next chapter, this finding fails.
findings.problem: The chapter's signature Yor/Anya collision arrives as a visual shock but never becomes locally legible as a true parallel operation or a decisive state change. Anya appears in field clothes at Dominic's estate, yet the chapter withholds what she was trying to do, what she has learned, and what concrete consequence follows from the encounter. Because the scene falls back to domestic pleasantries and exits on Yor's private summary, the promised collapse of family and mission boundaries is mostly deferred off-page.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:157 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:211 from Yor's POV so the scene exposes at least one concrete operational trace of Anya's presence at the compound, one observable sign that Anya now knows more than Yor can safely ignore, and one irreversible consequence or decision generated by their meeting before the chapter ends. Keep the restraint, but make the collision do chapter-level work on the page instead of asking Chapter 11 to explain it.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:121 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:149, the reader can identify the obstacle Yor is racing, why it buys only a limited window, and what concrete risk or compromise she incurs by staying. If she still captures the entire packet under nothing more urgent than ambient radio noise, this finding fails.
findings.problem: Once the gate radio and nearby buyers establish live pressure, the chapter still gives Yor a frictionless archive window: she lifts the second packet, recognizes the Garden code, reads a four-year pattern, links it to the port clerk, and photographs every page without meaningful interruption. The reveal depends on other people not reacting soon enough to Karl's silence, the downed corridor guards, or the continued delay.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:121 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:149 by adding a specific timer and cost. Options are flexible, but the passage needs either an explicit reason nobody checks on Karl yet or a sharper obstacle such as repeated radio traffic, approaching footsteps, a buyer entering the corridor, or a forced choice that prevents Yor from fully processing and photographing everything on site.

findings.acceptance_test: On a cold read, the first paragraph of Chapter 10 tells the reader where Yor's timeline sits relative to Damian and Anya's safe-house night before the compound action begins.
findings.evidence: Chapter 9 ends with Anya framing dawn as the next action point while Damian sleeps in the safe house; Chapter 10 then begins with Yor in a domestic setup and later reaches the compound at half past ten without saying whether this is earlier that same night, the next night, or a parallel rewind. snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2710 snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2712 snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2717 snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2773

evidence.excerpt: 2710: The bare bulb buzzed. His blood had dried brown at the edges of her nails from the butterfly strips. In her pocket, the folded note pressed against her thigh: `18:12 / west exit revision / WS-7`. The prepaid phone sat in the closet with its battery beside it, both of them waiting for morning. Dawn would be for moving him, reporting from somewhere cleaner, and starting with the one corridor she'd managed to narrow.

2712: For now he slept in brief, uneven drifts with his shoulder against hers and his fingers still slack in her sleeve. The missing name kept rising in him and disappearing before it formed. The ache under her ribs might have been only the baton bruise. It might not. She had no clean way to separate what belonged to her from what she'd taken. She stayed anyway.
2717: The milk was going to be a problem.

2773: Half past ten found her at the compound. The perimeter was a three-meter concrete wall topped with razor wire and motion sensors at the corners. Beyond it: the roofline of the main building, interior lights, the muted geometry of an estate that had been a manufacturing facility before Dominic repurposed it. Sealed loading docks on the east side. The main gate faced south, connected to the highway by a paved access road.

findings.problem: Because the boundary supplies no temporal marker, the reader has to stop and solve where Chapter 10 sits relative to the safe-house night. That reorientation work is visible because Chapter 9 has just pointed the reader toward dawn as the next beat, so the sudden reset to Yor's evening leaves the timeline briefly unstable.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tag Chapter 10's opening with an explicit temporal relation to Chapter 9, such as 'Earlier that evening' or 'The next night,' and keep one sentence that explains why the narrative is leaving the safe house now.

findings.acceptance_test: By the time Anya appears outside Dominic's compound, the reader can say what happened after the safe house and why this new location follows from the prior chapter's promise.

evidence.excerpt: 2710: The bare bulb buzzed. His blood had dried brown at the edges of her nails from the butterfly strips. In her pocket, the folded note pressed against her thigh: `18:12 / west exit revision / WS-7`. The prepaid phone sat in the closet with its battery beside it, both of them waiting for morning. Dawn would be for moving him, reporting from somewhere cleaner, and starting with the one corridor she'd managed to narrow.
2717: The milk was going to be a problem.
2877: Then the figure stepped into the spill of security light and it was Anya.

findings.problem: Chapter 9 ends by promising an immediate dawn sequence: move Damian, report from a cleaner line, and start working the WS-7 corridor. Chapter 10 then opens on Yor's kitchen and eventually reveals Anya at Karl Dominic's compound without cashing out that promised morning at all. Read back-to-back, the reader is left carrying a live procedural question across the boundary: what happened to Damian, and how did the safe-house lead turn into this new operation?
findings.rewrite_direction: Add a brief bridge in chapter 10 that situates Yor's night relative to the safe-house dawn, or backfill the handoff before Anya appears at the canal. One or two sentences are enough if they establish Damian's extraction/report status and what trail put Anya on Dominic.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
